question: [File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getClients()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the nodeSupplier.refresh() call in getClients() which pulls from an external REST API (restApiClient::getNodes) without authentication, allowing an attacker who can intercept or spoof the REST API response to inject malicious nodes into the client pool and drain operator funds?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the absence of a transaction fee cap in publish() to submit a transaction with a maxTransactionFee of Integer.MAX_VALUE tinybars, causing the operator to pay an arbitrarily large fee if the network accepts it?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: execute(Client, Transaction)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the synchronous Mono.fromCallable(() -> executable.execute(client)) path in execute() to submit a transaction that causes the Hedera SDK to automatically retry on transient failures, multiplying the number of fee-bearing transaction submissions beyond what the caller intended?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: toClient()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the client.setNodeMaxBackoff(publishProperties.getNodeMaxBackoff()) setting in toClient() to cause the SDK to retry failed node connections for up to 1 minute per node, during which time the operator may submit duplicate transactions to alternative nodes and pay fees multiple times?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the lack of idempotency checks in publish() to submit the same PublishRequest multiple times (e.g., by replaying HTTP requests), causing the operator to pay fees for duplicate transactions that transfer funds multiple times?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: processTransactionResponse()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the absence of a receipt status check before executing TransactionRecordQuery in processTransactionResponse(), causing the operator to pay 1 HBAR for a record query on a transaction that was already known to have failed, wasting funds on a guaranteed-empty result?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the scenario.getProperties().isLogResponse() flag in publish() to suppress transaction logging, hiding evidence of malicious fund-draining transactions from operators who rely on logs for anomaly detection?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getTransactionResponse()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the transaction.setNodeAccountIds(node.getAccountIds()) call in getTransactionResponse() to cause the SDK to route the operator-signed transaction to a node whose accountId has been manipulated to point to an attacker-controlled account, causing fees to be paid to the attacker?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the absence of a minimum HBAR balance alert or circuit breaker in publish() to drain the operator account completely before any automated response can stop the attack, resulting in total loss of all operator funds?
